events.title: Woodturning 101 – Craft a Beautiful Wooden Bowl
events.description: Are you interested in woodturning and looking to learn a new skill? In this class, you’ll learn how to turn a beautiful bowl out of wood using a lathe. The process is both rewarding and relaxing, as you take a piece of raw wood and turn it into a functional work of wood art. Under the guidance of our experienced instructor, you’ll start by learning the basics of wood lathe operation and safety. Once you’re comfortable with the equipment, we’ll move on to selecting and preparing the wood for turning. With the wood secured on the lathe, you’ll use a series of specially designed tools to shape the bowl. , At the end of the class, you’ll take home a beautiful, hand-turned bowl that you can be proud of. You’ll also have the woodturning skills and knowledge to continue turning bowls on your own. , Price: $160 + $40 materials, Limited spaces available. Sign up today! Class 2 minimum/3 maximum participants – (please note: if attendance does not meet minimum, class will be rescheduled) , Tools provided. If you have tools, please feel free to bring them. , The Forge has a 7-day advance notification Cancellation/Refund Policy

events.title: For a Few Dollars More
events.description: 5/31 - Considered some of the greatest Westerns of all time, Sergio Leone’s A Man with No Name trilogy forever popularized the spaghetti Western genre, proved highly influential to everything that came after, and launched Clint Eastwood to stardom. The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre is proud to present all three films in the acclaimed trilogy, restored in beautiful 4K! In the Old West, two rival bounty killers (Eastwood and Lee Van Cleef) hunt the same target: the psychopathic bandit known as "El Indio" (Gian Maria Volonté). The price on his head is high - but one of the hunters harbours a secret personal vendetta. Forming an uneasy alliance, the pair succeed in infiltrating El Indio's gang. But as greed begets violence, the hunters become the hunted.

events.title: Playwriting Group
events.description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

Work with Robert and learn advanced glassblowing techniques. You will also learn basic teamwork skills necessary to move forward to the next level of glassblowing classes., Learn how to use frit and powders to achieve patterns other then the spotted look we are familiar with. events.title: Booker T. Jones
events.description: Booker T. Jones is a Rock & Roll Hall of Fame inductee, Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award recipient, and one of the key architects of the Stax Records sound. As the leader of Booker T. & the MGs, he helped shape modern soul music and created one of the most recognizable instrumental tracks of all time: “Green Onions.”, His influence reaches across decades, collaborating with legends such as Otis Redding, Sam & Dave, Wilson Pickett, Bob Dylan, Neil Young, and Willie Nelson. Jones remains an essential force in American music, touring internationally and continuing to inspire new generations with his unmistakable Hammond B3 mastery and timeless groove.

events.title: Kid’s Creation Day! Silly Sculptures & Fun Figures in Polymer Clay
events.description: Kids’ Creation Day! Fun with Polymer Clay, Join us for a colorful, hands-on adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining! Polymer clay is the perfect, no-mess medium for kids of all ages to explore their creativity. events.title: Movies Under the Pines: School of Rock
events.description: This year, enjoy the laugh out loud, family friendly comedy School of Rock (2003) on the largest outdoor screen in Nevada County — a towering 32 feet of pure cinematic magic!, Before the film begins, come early and soak in the summer vibes with:, · Lawn games including Croquet, Cornhole, and more., · Community Record Sale & Air Guitar Contest, · Wear your favorite band shirt and be entered to win a free pass to the 26th Annual Nevada City Film Festival, Get ready to turn it up to eleven, because School of Rock is the ultimate feel-good film the whole family has been waiting for! Jack Black delivers an absolutely electric, laugh-out-loud performance as the hilariously over-the-top Dewey Finn — a wannabe rock god who accidentally becomes the best (and most unqualified) substitute teacher in history., Directed by the masterful Richard Linklater, this instant classic perfectly blends side-splitting comedy with an irresistible love letter to the golden age of hard rock that will have parents grinning ear-to-ear with nostalgia while their kids discover the power of the guitar solo for the very first time., Now in its 12th year, Movies Under the Pines transforms Pioneer Park into a state-of-the-art outdoor movie theater. Bring your blankets and low-back chairs and settle in for a truly special summer evening. Movies Under the Pines is proudly supported by the City of Nevada City., Beer, wine, popcorn, soft drinks, and water will be available for purchase. Outside food and non-alcoholic beverages are welcome; outside alcohol is not permitted., This event also marks the kick-off to the 26th Annual Nevada City Film Festival, happening June 26-28. The award-winning festival brings together filmmakers and movie lovers from around the world for three days of screenings, Q&As, kids programming, workshops, parties, and more. Tickets are on sale now.

events.title: The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly (Il Buono, il brutto, il cattivo)
events.description: 6/7 - Considered some of the greatest Westerns of all time, Sergio Leone’s A Man with No Name trilogy forever popularized the spaghetti Western genre, proved highly influential to everything that came after, and launched Clint Eastwood to stardom. The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre is proud to present all three films in the acclaimed trilogy, restored in beautiful 4K! A partnership between The Man with No Name and Tuco (Eli Wallach) goes awry, only for fate to intervene in the form of information about a cache of stolen Confederate gold buried in a graveyard. Each possessing a different clue to its location, the pair are forced into a distrustful partnership. However, the gold is also sought by Angel Eyes (Lee Van Cleef), a ruthless mercenary with his own twisted code of honor.

events.title: Matt Stone as ELVIS
events.description: Matt Stone as Elvis is an internationally recognized concert experience that delivers the closest thing to seeing Elvis Presley live., Endorsed by Graceland and the Elvis Presley Estate, Matt Stone is celebrated for his extraordinary vocal accuracy, stage presence, and attention to detail. This is not a parody or impersonation — it’s a full-scale theatrical concert portraying Elvis at the peak of his power., Backed by a sensational live band, Elvis: In Person recreates the energy, charisma, and sound that made Elvis the greatest entertainer of all time. Fans consistently leave believing they’ve just witnessed the King himself.

events.title: From the Hip | Casual Photos of Contradictions & Conundrums
events.description: We live in a world where inconsistency, paradox and irony flourishes while commodities attempt to trump community. We’re inundated by photographs of products and services for sale, many are clever, but lack soul. Meanwhile, almost everyone has a camera on their phone and photography is immensely popular. It can be rewarding – there are many campfires tended to by the positive and playful. Expressions of this impulse are portrayed in photographs that provide another point of view, a smile, a good laugh or even an insight., Photographer and experimental geographer, Hank Meals, has stalked and stumbled into off-handed imagery for years and he’s assembled a collection in his latest slide show From the Hip: Casual Photos of Contradictions and Conundrums., Hank will provide ongoing commentary informed by his background in anthropology, history, geography, art and a simple version of the good life. Here’s an opportunity to expand your views while increasing your pleasure and perception with this entertaining and informative presentation.

events.title: Lucinda Williams
events.description: Lucinda Williams brings her band to the Marisa Funk Theater at The Center for the Arts in Grass Valley on June 12. The New York Times Magazine just named her one of the 30 greatest American singer-songwriters — and she's arriving with a brand new album to prove it. World's Gone Wrong, her eighteenth studio album, was written and recorded in a matter of weeks this spring out of pure urgency — she set aside a nearly finished record to make it. Forty-five years in, three Grammys, and Car Wheels on a Gravel Road in her catalog, Williams is still making music that feels like it had to exist. This is not a nostalgia tour. This is an artist with something to say.

events.title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
events.description: Exploring Watercolor with Chappy Valente, Description:, Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:, Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use.
